title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam A
description: A 22-task RHCSA 10 mock exam covering boot recovery, networking, Flatpak management, systemd timers, LVM storage, firewall, SELinux, shell scripting, and chrony time synchronisation across client and server.

tasks:
Recover root access on client from the console.

Set the root password to: cinder9

Set the hostname on client to clienta.exam10.lab.
Add a persistent hosts entry so that servera.exam10.lab resolves to 192.168.122.3.

Configure the network connection for eth1 on client with the following settings:

IP Address: 192.168.122.60/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Method: manual
Autoconnect: yes

Configure the bootloader on client so every installed kernel boots with the kernel argument audit_backlog_limit=8192.

Requirements:
- The change must persist across reboots.
- Do not rely on a one-time GRUB edit.

Create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions on client using:

BaseOS: http://server/repo/BaseOS/
AppStream: http://server/repo/AppStream/
gpgcheck: disabled
Repositories: enabled

On server, create the same BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions:

BaseOS: http://server/repo/BaseOS/
AppStream: http://server/repo/AppStream/
gpgcheck: disabled
Repositories: enabled

On client, add a system-level Flatpak remote named examaflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled. Install org.rhcsa.Tools from that remote and leave it installed.

Create group opsa10 on client.
Create users anna10 and atlas10 with opsa10 as a supplementary group.
Set passwords for both users to cinder9.

On client, allow members of %opsa10 to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password prompt.
Use a sudoers drop-in file.
On client, set the maximum password age for anna10 to 45 days and the password warning period to 7 days.
Create an executable script /usr/local/bin/a-who on client that accepts a username as its first argument and prints that user's primary group name.
On client, write all usernames whose login shell ends with sh to /root/a-shell-users.txt, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
On client, create a gzip-compressed tar archive /root/a-etc.tar.gz containing /etc/hosts and /etc/fstab.

On client, create a regular file /root/a-original with some content.
Create a hard link /root/a-hard pointing to the same inode.
Create a symbolic link /root/a-soft pointing to /root/a-original.

On client, create a systemd timer unit examatimer.timer that triggers an associated examatimer.service every 10 minutes.
The service should run a one-shot script.
Enable the timer so it starts at boot.

On client, create volume group vga10 using /dev/sdb.
Create logical volume dataa of at least 384 MiB inside vga10.
Format it with XFS and mount it persistently at /mnt/dataa10.
On client, permanently allow TCP port 8100 through the firewall and reload firewalld so the rule takes effect at runtime.
On client, create the file /var/www/html/a.html and restore its default SELinux context so the type is httpd_sys_content_t.
On client, persistently enable the SELinux boolean httpd_can_network_connect.
On client, create the directory /srv/opsa10 owned by root:opsa10 with mode 3770 (setgid + sticky + rwx for owner and group).
On server, configure persistent systemd journal storage by setting Storage=persistent in journald.conf, create the /var/log/journal directory, and restart systemd-journald.

On client, configure the server (192.168.122.3) as the only chrony time source.
Remove all other pool/server lines.
Enable and start chronyd.

hints: Make all changes persistent across reboots unless a task explicitly states otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation only; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks are RHCSA 10 specific topics.
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c whoami 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root
hostnamectl --static | grep -qx clienta.exam10.lab && grep -Eq '^192\.168\.122\.3[[:space:]]+servera\.exam10\.lab' /etc/hosts

connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v d="$private_dev" '$2 == d {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.60/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
grubby --info=ALL | grep -q 'audit_backlog_limit=8192'

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; awk '"'"'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo'

flatpak remotes --system --columns=name,url 2>/dev/null | awk '$1 == "examaflatpak" && $2 == "file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo" {found=1} END {exit !found}' && flatpak list --system --app --columns=application 2>/dev/null | grep -qx org.rhcsa.Tools
getent group opsa10 >/dev/null && id -nG anna10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsa10 && id -nG atlas10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsa10
grep -ERq '^%opsa10[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d
chage -l anna10 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*45' && chage -l anna10 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7'
test -x /usr/local/bin/a-who && /usr/local/bin/a-who root | grep -qx root
test -s /root/a-shell-users.txt && grep -q '^root$' /root/a-shell-users.txt
test -f /root/a-etc.tar.gz && tar -tzf /root/a-etc.tar.gz | grep -q 'etc/hosts' && tar -tzf /root/a-etc.tar.gz | grep -q 'etc/fstab'
test -f /root/a-original && test "$(stat -c %i /root/a-original)" = "$(stat -c %i /root/a-hard)" && test -L /root/a-soft && readlink /root/a-soft | grep -q a-original
systemctl cat examatimer.timer >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl cat examatimer.timer | grep -q 'OnCalendar=.*0/10' && systemctl is-enabled examatimer.timer | grep -qx enabled

lv_path=/dev/vga10/dataa; mapper_path=/dev/mapper/vga10-dataa; mountpoint=/mnt/dataa10; lv_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$lv_path")"; lvs "$lv_path" >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET "$mountpoint" | grep -qx "$mountpoint" && awk -v dev="$lv_path" -v mapper="$mapper_path" -v uuid="$lv_uuid" -v target="$mountpoint" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == target && $3 == "xfs" && ($1 == dev || $1 == mapper || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8100/tcp && firewall-cmd --query-port=8100/tcp
test -f /var/www/html/a.html && ls -Z /var/www/html/a.html | grep -q httpd_sys_content_t
getsebool httpd_can_network_connect | grep -Eq -- '--> on$' && semanage boolean -l -C | grep -E '^httpd_can_network_connect\s+\(on\s*,\s*on\)'
stat -c '%G:%a' /srv/opsa10 | grep -qx opsa10:3770
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -d /var/log/journal && grep -Eq '"'"'^Storage=persistent$'"'"' /etc/systemd/journald.conf'
grep -Eq '^server[[:space:]]+server[[:space:]]+iburst' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Start with root recovery (task 1) since the root password is needed for all other tasks.
Configure networking and hostname early so server connectivity works for repo and chrony tasks.
Work through each question independently, verifying before moving on.
solution_commands: # Boot into emergency mode: at GRUB, press 'e', append rd.break to the linux line, Ctrl+X
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.60/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
